2604.01020.title: OrgAgent: Organize Your Multi-Agent System like a Company
2604.01020.paper_summary: While large language model-based multi-agent systems have shown strong potential for complex reasoning, how to effectively organize multiple agents remains an open question. In this paper, we introduce OrgAgent, a company-style hierarchical multi-agent framework that separates collaboration into governance, execution, and compliance layers. OrgAgent decomposes multi-agent reasoning into three layers: a governance layer for planning and resource allocation, an execution layer for task solving and review, and a compliance layer for final answer control. By evaluating the framework across reasoning tasks, LLMs, execution modes, and execution policies, we find that multi-agent systems organized in a company-style hierarchy generally outperform other organizational structures. Besides, hierarchical coordination also reduces token consumption relative to flat collaboration in most settings. For example, for GPT-OSS-120B, the hierarchical setting improves performance over flat multi-agent system by 102.73% while reducing token usage by 74.52% on SQuAD 2.0. Further analysis shows that hierarchy helps most when tasks benefit from stable skill assignment, controlled information flow, and layered verification. Overall, our findings highlight organizational structure as an important factor in multi-agent reasoning, shaping not only effectiveness and cost, but also coordination behavior.
2604.01020.paper_summary_zh: 雖然基於大型語言模型的多代理系統在複雜推理方面顯示出強大的潛力，但如何有效地組織多個代理仍然是一個未解的問題。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了OrgAgent，一種公司風格的分層多代理框架，將協作分為治理、執行和合規層。<lb/>OrgAgent將多代理推理分解為三個層次：治理層用於規劃和資源分配，執行層用於任務解決和審查，合規層用於最終答案控制。<lb/>通過在推理任務、LLMs、執行模式和執行政策上評估該框架，我們發現以公司風格層級組織的多代理系統通常優於其他組織結構。<lb/>此外，在大多數設置中，層級協調相較於平面協作也減少了標記消耗。<lb/>例如，對於GPT-OSS-120B，層級設置在SQuAD 2.0上將平面多代理系統的性能提高了102.73%，同時將標記使用量減少了74.52%。<lb/>進一步分析顯示，當任務受益於穩定的技能分配、受控的信息流和分層驗證時，層級結構的幫助最大。<lb/>總體而言，我們的研究結果強調了組織結構作為多代理推理中的一個重要因素，不僅影響有效性和成本，還影響協調行為。
2604.01020.author: Yiru Wang et.al.
2604.01020.authors: Yiru Wang, Xinyue Shen, Yaohui Han, Michael Backes, Pin-Yu Chen, Tsung-Yi Ho
